import math
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def read_sql(name):
    return (ROOT / "sql" / name).read_text(encoding="ascii")


def make_db():
    db = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    db.executescript(
        """
        CREATE TABLE length_unit (
            unit_code TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            centimeters_per_unit REAL NOT NULL CHECK (centimeters_per_unit > 0)
        );

        CREATE TABLE shipping_package (
            package_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            unit_code TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES length_unit(unit_code),
            length_value REAL NOT NULL CHECK (length_value >= 0),
            width_value REAL NOT NULL CHECK (width_value >= 0),
            height_value REAL NOT NULL CHECK (height_value >= 0)
        );
        """
    )
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO length_unit VALUES (?, ?)",
        [("CM", 1.0), ("IN", 2.54)],
    )
    db.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO shipping_package VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
        [
            (10, "IN", 10.0, 5.0, 2.0),
            (11, "IN", 20.0, 10.0, 8.0),
            (12, "IN", 19.0, 10.0, 8.0),
        ],
    )
    return db


def test_converts_every_dimension_before_computing_cubic_volume():
    db = make_db()
    row = db.execute(
        read_sql("package_volume.sql"),
        {"package_id": 10},
    ).fetchone()

    assert row[0] == 10
    assert math.isclose(row[1], 1638.7064, rel_tol=0.0, abs_tol=0.000001)


def test_oversize_queue_compares_converted_lengths_to_centimeters():
    db = make_db()
    rows = db.execute(
        read_sql("oversize_queue.sql"),
        {"limit_cm": 50.0},
    ).fetchall()

    assert rows == [(11,)]
